definition: The probability of a full credit-equity contagion chain completing to produce a severe outcome. Unconditional estimate ~35-40% based on 3/7 historical episodes producing severe outcomes. Conditional on operational Fed put ~20-25%; conditional on inflation-constrained Fed ~40-50%.
evidence: generalist_02 derives ~35-40% from transparent analogue decomposition: 3 severe in 7 episodes (1990, 2001-02, 2007-09)
generalist_01's 15-25% not transparently derived, appears deflated by conditioning on favorable Fed response
generalist_01 acknowledges probability rises to 35-50% if inflation blocks Fed, converging with generalist_02
Inflation constraint on Fed is the critical swing variable
open_questions: What is P(inflation constrains Fed | credit stress emerges)? — the most decision-relevant unknown
Is the historical analogue set (7 episodes) large enough for meaningful probability estimation?
How should structural changes (CET1, private credit) adjust the historical base rate?
